data.title: Okta rises after 'strong' Q3 results see largely positive reactions from analysts
data.description:
Okta's (NASDAQ:OKTA) stock rose about 3% on Wednesday after fiscal third-quarter results and outlook surpassed estimates and Wall Street analysts largely maintained positive views.

JMP Securities said it maintained its Market Perform rating on Okta, after the company reported strong third quarter results for fiscal 2025, with non-GAAP EPS of $0.67 (consensus $0.58) on revenue of about $655.0M, up 14% year-over-year (consensus $649.3M), and an operating margin of 21% (consensus 18.4%).

Analysts led by Trevor Walsh added that Okta's Co-Founder and CEO Todd McKinnon commented on the earnings call that "new products are starting to blossom and have the potential to be significant contributors [as] Governance now [represents] 30% of the value of deals it's included in."

UBS kept its Buy rating and $108 price target on Okta's stock.

Analyst Roger Boyd and his team said that fiscal third quarter results were healthy across the board, with current remaining performance obligations, or cRPO, growth of 13% (a 4 point beat versus guidance, the largest since fourth quarter fiscal 2024), total revenue growth of 14%, 21% operating margin, all ahead of expectations.

The analysts added that Okta attributed this largely to better execution versus the second quarter — not any improvement in macro or seat/MAU headwinds — and to a lesser extent, to greater than expected second half weighting in fiscal 2025.

Okta also sounded more confident in the growth trajectory, having lapped the October 2023 security incident with limited impact, according to the analysts. Looking forward to fiscal 2026, Okta provided an initial outlook for 7% revenue growth (below consensus) and profitability guidance of at least 22% operating margin and at least 24% free cash flow margin (in-line with consensus), the analysts added.

In addition, Walsh and his team said they still see more upside to the shares from here.

Bernstein maintained its Market-Perform rating on Okta and increased the price target on the shares to $123 from $116.

Analyst Peter Weed and his team said that Okta's fiscal third quarter results delivered another quarter of low-to-mid 2% beat versus guidance, and full year was raised by more than twice the beat (again).

However, the analysts added that growth rate continues to decelerate. Net Revenue Retention, or NRR, dropped again, -200 bps to 108% versus 110% last quarter (down more than -1,000bps over the last two years), according to the analysts. The company guided it to decline again next quarter (the analysts think this is conservative, and model it flat for the next few quarters).

The analysts said that it is not only existing customer expansion that remains weak, net new customer numbers of +150 quarter-over-quarter remains less than half the quarterly additions that were being regularly delivered as recently as a year ago (same quarter a year ago was +400 quarter-over-quarter). The company agreed this remains well below their aspiration, the analysts noted.

Related stocks: Palo Alto Networks (PANW), CrowdStrike (CRWD) and Fortinet (FTNT) were up about 2% on Wednesday.

data.title: Trimble upgraded to Overweight due to cyclical hardware recovery: JP Morgan
data.description:
Trimble (NASDAQ:TRMB) was upgraded ahead of its investor day to Overweight from Neutral by J.P. Morgan as the investment firm expects "cyclical hardware recovery and stable software growth."

J.P. Morgan also increased its price target on the stock to $92 from $74. Trimble shares inched up 1% during early market trading on Wednesday. Shares are up more than 20% since its third quarter earnings call in early November.

Trimble produces hardware and software for an array of industries, including construction, agriculture and transportation. The Sunnyvale, Calif.-based tech company is holding its annual investor day on December 10.

"We expect TRMB to provide long term financial targets through 2029 (similar to the 5-year outlook provided in 2022) including an organic revenue CAGR of HSD% at the mid-point, LDD% ARR growth (mid-teens in the earlier years), adjusted EBITDA margin of low 30%, and EPS growth algo of LDD% with potential for further upside through M&A and buybacks," said J.P. Morgan analysts, led by Tami Zakaria, in a Wednesday note.

Seeking Alpha contributor Hunter Wolf Research recently assigned Trimble a Buy rating and an $86 price target due to the company's shift to software-driven models and double-digit ARR growth.

"Bringing all the segments together, I calculate Trimble will grow its revenue by 8% organically," he said. "Additionally, I project the company will allocate 6% of revenue toward M&A, contributing 2.4% growth to the topline."

data.title: IPO Roundup: Fast Track Group, MasterBeef and more
data.description:
The U.S. IPO calendar looks muted as the last month of the year starts. Compared to December, January and February have been historically stronger months for new listings.

Last week, a numbers for companies priced their respective IPOs. This included autonomous vehicle technology firm Pony AI (PONY), beauty and e-commerce platform Nice One Beauty Digital Marketing Company, explorer Brazil Potash (GRO), and meat processor Wing Yip Food (WYHG), among others.

Investing Group Leader Donovan Jones said in an analysis of Wing Yip Food, "The market opportunity for selling processed meat products in China is large and expected to grow at a moderate rate of growth in the coming years and is characterized by high fragmentation and significant regional differences."

"Risks to the company’s outlook as a public company include its "foreign private issuer" and "emerging growth company" status, which will enable management to disclose substantially less information to shareholders."

Upcoming IPOs/SPACs that are expected to be priced this week

  • Fast Track Group (FTRK) is raising $13.5M by offering 3.0M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Event management company.
  • Jupiter Neurosciences (JUNS) is raising $11.0M by offering 2.8M shares at a price of about $4.00. Description: Clinical research and development pharmaceutical company.
  • Tavia Acquisition Corp. (TAVIU) is raising $100.0M by offering 10.0M shares at a price of about $10.00. Description: Blank check company.
  • Jinxin Technology (NAMI) is raising $8.4M by offering 1.9M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Digital content service provider in China.
  • NetClass Technology (NTCL) is raising $9.0M by offering 1.8M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $6.00. Description: SaaS subscription services provider.
  • ALE Group Holdings (ALEH) is raising $6.3M by offering 1.3M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Accounting and corporate consulting services provider.
  • Fitness Champs Holdings (FCHL) is raising $9.0M by offering 2.0M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Sports education provider in Singapore.

Filing, terms, and changes

  • Range Capital Acquisition (RANGU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $100M in an initial public offering. Description: Blank check company.
  • Inflection Point Acquisition III (IPCXU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $220M in an initial public offering. Description: Blank check company.
  • GATES GROUP (GTSG) has filed and set terms with the SEC to raise up to $15M in an initial public offering. The company is looking to offer 2.5M shares at a price range of about $5.50 and $6.50. Description: Japanese real estate company.
  • Top Win (TOPW) has filed and set terms with the SEC to raise up to $13M in an initial public offering. The company is looking to offer 2.7M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Hong Kong luxury watch wholesaler.
  • MasterBeef (MB) has set terms with the SEC to raise up to $9M in an initial public offering. The company is looking to offer 2.0M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Hong Kong restaurant group.

Since the start of the year, the Renaissance IPO ETF (NYSEARCA:IPO) has risen about 23.1.%, slightly underperforming the S&P 500 index, which has gained about 26.8%.

data.title: U.S. factory orders rose slightly less than expected in October
data.description:
U.S. factory orders rose 0.2% M/M to $586.7B in October, compared with the 0.4% increase and September's 0.2% retreat (revised from -0.5%).

Factory order excluding transportation: +0.1% vs. +0.1% prior.

Unfilled orders, up fifty of the last fifty-one months, increased $6.1 billion or 0.4 percent to $1,398.8 billion. This followed a 0.3 percent September increase.

The unfilled orders-to-shipments ratio was 7.03, up from 6.97 in September.

Inventories, down two consecutive months, decreased $0.4 billion or 0.1 percent to $856.8 billion. This followed a 0.3 percent September decrease. The inventories-to-shipments ratio was 1.46, unchanged from September, the Census Bureau said on Wednesday.

data.title: Lilly, Rondo to collaborate on CD28 bispecific antibody development
data.description:
• Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has entered into a collaboration with Rondo Therapeutics for the development of CD28 bispecific antibodies for the treatment of solid tumors.
  • Under the deal, Rondo will receive an upfront cash payment, plus potential milestone payments and tiered royalties on future product sales.
  • Lilly (LLY) will be given exclusive worldwide rights to develop a CD28-co-stimulatory therapeutic for a defined cancer target, according to a statement.
  • Details of the deal were not disclosed.
  • Earlier Wednesday, Lilly (LLY) announced that a head-to-head study found patients lost more weight on average taking Lilly’s (LLY) weight-loss drug Zepbound than Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) Wegovy.

data.title: Cabot Corporation announces 3-year financial targets
data.description:
• Cabot Corporation (NYSE:CBT) (NYSE: CBT) expects adjusted EPS CAGR of 7-10% from fiscal 2024 through fiscal 2027.
  • The company sees adjusted EBITDA of $0.9-$1.0B by fiscal year 2027.
  • CBT shares were up 1% on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: France’s political crisis-Impact of no-confidence motion on markets
data.description:
The French government is on the brink of a collapse as the Parliament sets to vote on a no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Michel Barnier, which can plunge the eurozone’s second-biggest economy into a deeper political crisis.

The challenge to Barnier’s government stems from opposition to his proposed budget to reduce France’s massive fiscal deficit. If the motion passes, it would mark the first time in over 60 years that a French government has been ousted through such a motion.

This follows months of political uncertainty in France, starting when President Emmanuel Macron called for a snap election in June, which resulted in a hung parliament.

The political turmoil has varying implications for both French and European markets, triggering volatility in both. France's benchmark index CAC 40 (CAC:IND) has shown significant weakness lately, declining over 9% since Macron called for a snap election.

The euro (EUR:USD) has also declined sharply against the U.S. dollar, last down 0.16%. French government bonds are also seeing higher yields as investors demand higher returns for potential risks. The yield spread between French and German 10-year government bonds reached 90 bps last week, its highest level since 2012.

France’s political crisis has reached beyond its borders, harboring negative sentiment across European markets, with bearish trading signals for European indices. The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) has also ticked lower, weighed down by volatility in France’s CAC 40 Index.

If the no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Barnier passes, it would mean increased volatility in markets, the immediate concern being the passage of the French budget.

The move could also rattle currency markets, with the euro already faltering against the U.S. Dollar. The European Central Bank (ECB)’s monetary policies will also be influenced by the uncertain market sentiment.

If the motion fails to pass, investors would not necessarily be calmed, as it might only provide temporary relief without addressing the underlying political issues.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: CHWY, CRM, MRVL, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher on Wednesday following the release of the October JOLTS report, which revealed a rise in job openings to 7.744M, exceeding the 7.490M consensus. Market participants are now keenly awaiting Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech later today for further insights on the direction of interest rates.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Salesforce (CRM) shares soared nearly 11% despite mixed FQ3 results, driven by consistent revenue and subscription growth of 8% and 9% YoY, respectively. For FQ4, Salesforce guided revenue of $9.9B–$10.1B (vs. $10.5B consensus) and EPS of $2.57–$2.62 (vs. $2.65 consensus). For FY2025, the company expects revenue of $37.8B–$38B (midpoint: $37.9B, slightly above $37.86B consensus) and EPS of $9.98–$10.03 (midpoint: $10.01, below $10.11 consensus).
  • Shares of Marvell Technology (MRVL) jumped 11% after reporting strong FQ3 results and an upbeat FQ4 outlook, driven by artificial intelligence. Data center revenue surged 98% Y/Y. For Q4, Marvell expects revenue of $1.8B (+/- 5%) vs. $1.65B consensus, with gross margins of ~50% and adjusted margins of ~60%. It expects adjusted EPS of $0.59 (+/- $0.05) vs. the consensus of $0.52 for Q4. CEO Matt Murphy highlighted 19% sequential revenue growth for Q4 and projected FY2026 momentum, calling this 'the beginning of a new era of growth for Marvell.'
  • Okta (OKTA) shares surged 15% after delivering strong fiscal Q3 results and raising its FY2025 guidance. For Q4, the company projects adjusted EPS of $0.73–$0.74 (vs. $0.67 consensus) and revenue of $667M–$669M (vs. $650.7M consensus). For FY2025, Okta raised its adjusted EPS forecast to $2.75–$2.76 (up from $2.58–$2.63 and vs. $2.61 consensus) and revenue to $2.595B–$2.597B (up from $2.56B–$2.57B), representing 15% YoY growth.

Biggest stock losers

  • Gaotu Techedu (GOTU) shares plunged over 9% following FQ3 results as escalating losses overshadowed a 53% Y/Y increase in revenue and a 67.2% rise in gross billings to RMB1,069.2M. The company reported a sharp rise in net loss to RMB471.3M compared to RMB57.7M in 3Q23, with gross profit margin falling to 64.4% from 72.4% a year ago. Operating expenses surged 89.1% Y/Y, driven by increased marketing efforts and workforce expansion.
  • Shares of Chewy (CHWY) tumbled over 10% as weaker-than-expected FQ3 profit, a decline in active customers, and slowing spending overshadowed its upbeat FY24 guidance. Profit fell by 4 cents to $0.20 per share from the previous quarter, gross margin narrowed by 20 basis points, and net spending per active customer rose by just $2, signaling waning momentum. Despite this, Chewy forecasted FQ4 sales of $3.18B-$3.20B (above $3.17B consensus) and FY24 revenue of $11.79B-$11.81B (above $11.76B consensus).
  • Intuitive Machines (LUNR) lost 14% after the company priced its upsized public offering of 9.52M Class A common stock shares at $10.50/share. Underwriters granted an option to purchase an additional 1.28M shares from the company and 0.15M shares from a selling stockholder. Separately, the company agreed to issue 952,381 Class A shares to Boryung Corporation at the offering price in a private placement contingent upon certain closing conditions.

data.title: ISM Services PMI slips more than expected in November
data.description:
• ISM US Services PMI: 52.1 vs. 55.5 consensus and 56.0 in October.
  • Prices: 58.2 vs 56.4 consensus and 58.1 prior.
  • Employment: 51.5 vs 53.0 consensus and 53.0 prior.
  • New orders: 53.7  vs. 56.6 consensus and 57.4 prior.

data.title: TotalEnergies confirms purchase of German renewables developer VSB in $1.6B deal
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) said Wednesday it agreed to acquire German renewable project developer VSB Group from P-E firm Partners Group Holding for €1.57B (~$1.6B), adding more than 2 GW of developed onshore wind power capacity across Europe.

VSB has over 475 MW of renewable capacity in operation or under construction mainly in Germany and France, and a pipeline of 18 GW of wind, solar and battery storage technologies mainly across Germany, Poland and France.

TotalEnergies (TTE) currently has 24 GW of gross installed renewable capacity, with targets that call for adding 11 GW by next year and reaching 100 GW by 2030.

At the same time, TotalEnergies (TTE) said it agreed to sell a 50% stake in a 2 GW portfolio of solar and battery power projects in the Texas ERCOT market to funds managed by Apollo Global Management (APO) for $800M in cash.

The portfolio consists of three solar projects with a combined capacity of 1.7 GW and two battery-energy storage projects with a capacity of 300 MW; TotalEnergies (TTE) will remain operator of the assets and retain a 50% stake.
